methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Nascent Materials is a seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $2.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private frontier-tech companies and one public comparable (LNZA), representing the frontier energy and materials subsector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $2.3M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $5.75M. Peer-median analysis references three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—with a median valuation of $150M and a range of $110M to $200M. These peers represent established positions in climate-adjacent infrastructure and materials science.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $5M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $78M. The wide spread reflects the fundamental tension between stage-based anchoring, which suggests a near-term post-seed valuation in the $5–6M range, and peer comparable positioning, which assumes Nascent Materials reaches near-series A performance parity with the reference set. Key uncertainties include: (1) whether the peer set adequately reflects Nascent's specific technical risk and market timing, and (2) the extent to which seed-stage capital efficiency predicts eventual comparability to later-stage peers. A defensible valuation would likely weight stage-progression more heavily absent evidence of exceptional technical or market traction.
